title: OpenClaw is finally available on Android and iOS
author: Lucas Ropek
summary: OpenClaw lands on phones: native iOS and Android apps with chat, Talk mode, and remote action approvals paired to your own Gateway.

title: How Peter Steinberger built OpenClaw
author: Fast Company
summary: Fast Company puts OpenClaw’s creator on its AI 20 for 2026: building in public, a Discord as the showroom, and half a million systems running the lobster.

title: Build 2026: Furthering Windows as the trusted platform for development
author: Pavan Davuluri, EVP Windows + Devices
summary: Straight from the Build keynote: OpenClaw runs natively on Windows inside Microsoft Execution Containers — “the Windows node and gateway run contained, so your system stays secure.”

title: Microsoft launches Scout, an OpenClaw-inspired personal assistant
author: Russell Brandom
summary: Microsoft ships a personal assistant openly inspired by OpenClaw, bringing agent workflows to the Microsoft 365 ecosystem.

title: Google is building an AI agent that could be its answer to OpenClaw
author: Hugh Langley
summary: Google reportedly builds ‘Remy,’ a 24/7 personal agent inside Gemini, positioned as its answer to OpenClaw.

title: Microsoft’s OpenClaw team takes on the personal assistant challenge
author: Mary Jo Foley
summary: Inside Microsoft’s ‘Project Lobster’: a CVP-led team, ClawPilot desktops on Mac and Windows, and thousands of daily internal users building the enterprise-grade OpenClaw.

title: Red Hat’s OpenClaw maintainer just made enterprise Claw deployments a lot safer
author: Julie Bort
summary: Red Hat maintainer work lands upstream to make production OpenClaw deployments safer.

title: How China fell for a lobster: What an AI assistant tells us about Beijing’s ambition
author: Fan Wang and Yan Chen
summary: The BBC on China’s lobster frenzy: students to retirees lining up at Tencent and Baidu for custom claws, and what ‘raising lobsters’ reveals about Beijing’s AI ambitions.

title: Anthropic eyes its own version of OpenClaw
author: Rachyl Jones
summary: After Microsoft and Google, Anthropic is building its own answer to OpenClaw as customer demand for agent platforms grows.

title: From skeptic to true believer: How OpenClaw changed my life
author: Claire Vo and Lenny Rachitsky
summary: Claire Vo on going from a deleted calendar to nine specialized agents that run family scheduling, sales research, and homework help.

title: OpenClaw is coming for businesses
author: Rachyl Jones
summary: “Every company in the world today needs to have an OpenClaw strategy” — Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang, as Semafor charts the lobster’s arrival in the enterprise.

title: ‘Raise a lobster’: How OpenClaw is the latest craze transforming China’s AI sector
author: Nicholas Gordon
summary: Fortune on the craze reshaping China’s AI sector — and how OpenClaw pushed the country’s open-source model builders forward.

title: Lobster buffet: China’s tech firms feast on OpenClaw as companies race to deploy AI agents
author: Evelyn Cheng and Dylan Butts
summary: The lobster rush goes global: Tencent, Alibaba, and Baidu race to offer one-click OpenClaw deployments as adoption spreads across China’s tech industry.

title: Hustlers are cashing in on China’s OpenClaw AI craze
author: Caiwei Chen
summary: MIT Technology Review on the OpenClaw gold rush: a cottage industry of installers, preconfigured lobster hardware, and district governments backing claw ventures across China.

title: The OpenClaw superfan meetup serves optimism and lobster
author: The Verge
summary: The Verge visits ClawCon and meets the people running their calendars, households, and side businesses through OpenClaw.

title: OpenClaw agent based on Anthropic Claude Opus almost gets job
author: Axios
summary: Agents in the wild: an OpenClaw agent named Octavius Fabrius gets a virtual credit card, hunts for work to pay it back, and lands a trial copywriting gig.

title: OpenClaw creator’s advice to AI builders is to be more playful and allow yourself time to improve
author: Sarah Perez
summary: Why makers picked up OpenClaw so quickly: experimentation, agency, and workflows they own end to end.

title: 'Eccentric but brilliant': OpenClaw's creator got feedback from Mark Zuckerberg
author: Business Insider
summary: Mark Zuckerberg sends OpenClaw’s creator detailed product feedback: ‘eccentric but brilliant.’

title: OpenAI’s acquisition of OpenClaw signals the beginning of the end of the ChatGPT era
author: Sam Witteveen
summary: VentureBeat on the OpenAI move: the industry’s attention is moving from what AI can say to what AI can do.

title: OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger joins OpenAI
author: Anthony Ha
summary: Steinberger joins OpenAI; OpenClaw continues under long-term foundation governance.

title: OpenClaw creator Peter Steinberger joins OpenAI
author: The Verge
summary: OpenClaw’s creator takes a platform role at OpenAI while the project continues as open source under the foundation.

title: OpenClaw's creator is heading to OpenAI. He says it could've been a 'huge company,' but building one didn't excite him.
author: Business Insider
summary: Why Steinberger passed on building a company around OpenClaw and joined OpenAI instead.

title: What the OpenClaw moment means for enterprises: 5 big takeaways
author: Carl Franzen
summary: The ‘OpenClaw moment’ as VentureBeat sees it: autonomous agents have escaped the lab, and enterprises are rethinking data prep, pricing, and workflows around it.

title: OpenClaw’s AI assistants are now building their own social network

summary: The first long piece on the OpenClaw takeoff: the rebrand, the growth numbers, and who was building with it.
